tweets.text:
Trump tweeting up a storm today on TS, now slamming communism 

"I will be speaking at 1:30 P.M. to The Faith and Freedom Coalition, and one of the Statements I will be making, perhaps the most important of them all, concerns the recent Election of Communists in our Country. Communism is very easy to sell. I’d be the Greatest Communist in History. I’d give free rent, free houses, free food, everything is free. Unfortunately, after two or three years, the Country where this is taking place would fail. It always does, and then you’ll start living in squalor" lots more text follows...

tweets.text:
*KASHKARI: AI PROBABLY PUSHING UP MARKET INTEREST RATES

So, record flood of IG debt to fund hyperscalers capex is raising cost of capital for everyone else. In other words, AI has displaced Fed as source of global credit impulse.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
After covid, trillions in government debt issuance sent global inflation soaring, and unleashed a massive worldwide credit impulse.

Now, as part of AI capex cycle, hundreds of billions in corporate debt are sending chips-related inflation soaring, sparking a new credit impulse
tweets.full_text:
*KASHKARI: AI PROBABLY PUSHING UP MARKET INTEREST RATES

So, record flood of IG debt to fund hyperscalers capex is raising cost of capital for everyone else. In other words, AI has displaced Fed as source of global credit impulse.

tweets.text:
Retail is piling into chip stocks:

Retail investors have purchased a massive +$22.5 billion of US-listed semiconductor ETFs year-to-date.

This figure has surged more than +1,000% since early April.

Over the last month alone, retail purchases rose to +$12.0 billion, the highest monthly purchase on record.

To put this into perspective, monthly retail purchases of US-listed semiconductor ETFs did not exceed +$2.0 billion in any month of 2024 and 2025.

Meanwhile, retail has spent an average of $1.9 billion per day on semiconductor options contracts so far in June, up +16% from the previous record of $1.6 billion set in May.

Retail investors are chasing the chip rally.
tweets.full_text:
Retail is piling into chip stocks:

Retail investors have purchased a massive +$22.5 billion of US-listed semiconductor ETFs year-to-date.

This figure has surged more than +1,000% since early April.

Over the last month alone, retail purchases rose to +$12.0 billion, the highest monthly purchase on record.

To put this into perspective, monthly retail purchases of US-listed semiconductor ETFs did not exceed +$2.0 billion in any month of 2024 and 2025.

Meanwhile, retail has spent an average of $1.9 billion per day on semiconductor options contracts so far in June, up +16% from the previous record of $1.6 billion set in May.

Retail investors are chasing the chip rally.

tweets.full_text: *Fixed labels https://t.co/oPYgPMnNxS

tweets.text:
*Hormuz Crossing Update, 6/26/26* 

🚢Vessels are still transiting the Strait of Hormuz, but the rebound has stalled. The latest 24-hour rolling count shows 50 commercial crossings — 16 eastbound and 34 westbound — up from the collapse, but still far below the 100-plus levels seen before the disruption. 

Security risk remains elevated: following reports that a cargo vessel near the Oman coast was struck by Iran’s Revolutionary Guards, damaging the bridge but causing no injuries, while the U.S. and Oman continue to push back against any transit-fee regime for the Strait. In short, traffic has resumed, but confidence has not fully returned.

*📡Powered by our commercial crossing index (AIS data refreshed every 30 mins), major real-time chokepoint trackers availible on @TheTerminal.

tweets.text:
Good Morning from Germany, where the heatwave is already hitting the country’s economic arteries. Rhine water levels at Kaub – a key bottleneck on Europe’s industrial waterway – have fallen 0.20m in just 3 days to around 0.83m, the lowest since April 2025.
That matters: the Rhine is a crucial transport route for Germany’s chemical industry, including BASF, Covestro and Evonik. Low water can restrict barge loads, raise logistics risks and disrupt supply chains. For now, barge rates to Basel are still flat – but the heat is on.

tweets.text:
Stocks go up, everyone's bullish. Stocks go down, everyone's bearish.

This is the most common way to read the economy.

The market does lead the broad economy by a couple of months. 

But it lags the part that turns first, and that's the part worth watching.

We now borrow most of what we invest from the rest of the world. https://t.co/tgzbf19HOV

tweets.text:
If there is a limit to how much memory can be produced, then there is a limit to how many GPUs can be produced, which means that there’s a limit to how many data centers can be built.

This is bad news for some auxiliary AI plays such as companies making power generation equipment. 

It’s not entirely bad news for the hyperscalers, however. While the memory shortage raises their costs, it also keeps the demand for compute above the available supply, which gives them greater pricing power over access to cloud computing.

thread_tweets.text:
Know which assets are moving higher.

The TC screen updates daily — which tokens just flipped to bull, which just dropped. Today: DEXE and UNI promoted, NEAR dropped. Bull breadth across the top 100: 8%.

RV community wanted to not have to scan 100 charts. So the screen does it. Its also shows the historical performance of the indicator on every asset selected. The indicator outperforms in aggregate, but some assets' volatility and trend profiles are perfect. This will surfact that.
thread_tweets.full_text:
Know which assets are moving higher.

The TC screen updates daily — which tokens just flipped to bull, which just dropped. Today: DEXE and UNI promoted, NEAR dropped. Bull breadth across the top 100: 8%.

RV community wanted to not have to scan 100 charts. So the screen does it. Its also shows the historical performance of the indicator on every asset selected. The indicator outperforms in aggregate, but some assets' volatility and trend profiles are perfect. This will surfact that.

thread_tweets.text:
Know which assets are outperforming the benchmark or current high flyer.

The relative momentum screen compares any token to any benchmark: BTC, ETH, SOL, HYPE. Gaining ground or giving it back? That question matters in a bull market; it becomes a sorting function, critical for allocating finite capital.
thread_tweets.full_text:
Know which assets are outperforming the benchmark or current high flyer.

The relative momentum screen compares any token to any benchmark: BTC, ETH, SOL, HYPE. Gaining ground or giving it back? That question matters in a bull market; it becomes a sorting function, critical for allocating finite capital.

thread_tweets.text:
Know the regime. Know the probabilities.

The BTC trend filter is the gate. Below the market filter, no longs. Risk off since May 17. Bull breadth at 8%. This defines the regime where the historical probabilities favour engaging.

Trade when the filter says no can still generate outsized returns (e.g., HYPE YTD). But trade when the filter says yes; overall, hit rate and profit factor jump higher. 

RV sale this week : https://t.co/IEyOp5pgbR

tweets.full_text:
In the next week, new dashboards are coming to @RealVision.

This is the Trend/Momentum indicator that members access via Trading View, operationalised as a live screening tool across the Crypto Top 100. Single-asset absolute screens. Relative momentum against any benchmark: BTC, ETH, SOL, HYPE. Risk filters are built in.

Trend-following, made accessible.

Know which assets are moving higher.

The TC screen updates daily — which tokens just flipped to bull, which just dropped. Today: DEXE and UNI promoted, NEAR dropped. Bull breadth across the top 100: 8%.

RV community wanted to not have to scan 100 charts. So the screen does it. Its also shows the historical performance of the indicator on every asset selected. The indicator outperforms in aggregate, but some assets' volatility and trend profiles are perfect. This will surfact that.

Know which assets are outperforming the benchmark or current high flyer.

The relative momentum screen compares any token to any benchmark: BTC, ETH, SOL, HYPE. Gaining ground or giving it back? That question matters in a bull market; it becomes a sorting function, critical for allocating finite capital.

Know the regime. Know the probabilities.

The BTC trend filter is the gate. Below the market filter, no longs. Risk off since May 17. Bull breadth at 8%. This defines the regime where the historical probabilities favour engaging.

Trade when the filter says no can still generate outsized returns (e.g., HYPE YTD). But trade when the filter says yes; overall, hit rate and profit factor jump higher.
